identity.short_name: Mobius inversion with a two-sided valuation twist feeding an LTE plus CRT multiplicative-order computation

problem_spec.summary: On the divisor lattice of $n_0=p^k q^2$, define a valuation-sensitive function $F$ that adds one twist on multiples of $q$ and a second twist on multiples of $p$. Recover $f(n_0)$ uniquely via Mobius inversion, extract the $p$-adic factor $t=v_p(f(n_0))$ and $M=f(n_0)/p^t$, then compute the multiplicative order of $M$ modulo $n_0$ by combining LTE-based order lifting on $p^k$ and $q^2$ with a CRT-lcm recombination.
givens: $p$ and $q$ are distinct odd primes and $k\ge 3$

For each divisor $n\mid n_0$ with $i=v_p(n)$ and $j=v_q(n)$, define $F$ by the rules: $F(1)=1$; if $j=0$ and $i\ge 1$ then $F(p^i)=p^i+Bp^{i-1}$; if $i=0$ and $j\ge 1$ then $F(q^j)=q^j+Aq^{j-1}$; if $i\ge 1$ and $j\ge 1$ then $F(p^i q^j)=p^i q^j + A p^i q^{j-1} + B p^{i-1} q^j$
There exists an arithmetic function $f$ on divisors of $n_0$ such that for every $n\mid n_0$, $F(n)=\sum_{d\mid n} f(d)$
ask: Determine the least positive integer $r$ such that $M^r\equiv 1(\mathrm{mod}\ n_0)$

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: base prime for the $p^k$ component and $p$-adic order lifting

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: base prime for the $q^2$ component and $q$-adic order lifting

invariant(s).name: convolution_encoding
invariant(s).statement: $F(n)=\sum_{d\mid n} f(d)$ is equivalent to $F=f*\mathbf{1}$ under Dirichlet convolution on divisors of $n_0$

invariant(s).name: mobius_inverse_of_1
invariant(s).statement: $\mu*\mathbf{1}=\varepsilon$ on the divisor lattice, so $f$ is uniquely determined by $f=F*\mu$

invariant(s).name: squarefree_reduction_for_p_k_q2
invariant(s).statement: For $n_0=p^k q^2$, only squarefree divisors contribute in Mobius inversion, so $f(n_0)=F(n_0)-F(n_0/p)-F(n_0/q)+F(n_0/(pq))$

invariant(s).name: LTE_valuation_growth
invariant(s).statement: For an odd prime $\ell$ with $\ell\mid (a-1)$, LTE gives $v_\ell(a^{\ell^m}-1)=v_\ell(a-1)+m$

essential_assumption(s): Mobius inversion applies on the divisor lattice, so $F=f*\mathbf{1}$ implies $f=F*\mu$
The computed $M$ is coprime to $n_0$, so its multiplicative order modulo $n_0$ is defined
$p$ and $q$ are odd primes so LTE applies in the form used
uniqueness_certificate(s): Mobius inversion uniquely determines $f(n_0)$ and therefore uniquely determines $t$ and $M$
Lemma 2 fixes the local orders from $v_p(M-1)$ and $v_q(M-1)$, and Lemma 3 then fixes the global order $r$ uniquely

technique(s).name: Dirichlet_convolution_optional
technique(s).purpose: encode the hypothesis as a convolution equation

technique(s).name: multiplicative_functions
technique(s).purpose: evaluate $F$ on the required divisors and simplify

technique(s).name: lifting_the_exponent_LTE
technique(s).purpose: compute local orders modulo prime powers

technique(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
technique(s).purpose: recombine local orders into the global order using $\mathrm{lcm}$

theorem(s).name: chinese_remainder_theorem
theorem(s).role: supports the order-lcm relation for coprime moduli

theorem(s).name: euler_theorem
theorem(s).role: guarantees existence of some $r>0$ with $M^r\equiv 1(\mathrm{mod}\ n_0)$ when $\mathrm{gcd}(M,n_0)=1$

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Divisor-sum transforms are inverted by Mobius inclusion-exclusion
Orders modulo coprime moduli are controlled by prime-power behavior and recombined by $\mathrm{lcm}$

critical_trick(s): Use squarefree collapse to compute $f(n_0)$ from four values of $F$
Use LTE to obtain exact local orders from $v_p(M-1)$ and $v_q(M-1)$, then use CRT-lcm to obtain the global order

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: gcd and pow for modular checks

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: mobius and multiplicative order computations for independent verification

task(s).name: independent_verification
task(s).reason: Compute $f(n_0)$ by Mobius inversion, extract $t$ and $M$, then compute the order of $M$ modulo $n_0$ and confirm minimality
